title: Cables, Connectors, Adapters, Interfaces, and Capability Negotiation · Causal Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs cables, connectors, adapters, interfaces, and capability negotiation from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for connector and end-to-end capability map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.
Compare category, conductor, shielding, length, bend, rating, directionality, active components, markings, and environmental suitability.
Distinguish passive pin adaptation from active protocol conversion and account for direction, resolution, bandwidth, audio, copy protection, and power.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Connector Identification · changed case · Review Connector Identification
steps.instruction:
review case — Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. stimulus.title: Signal Path Test case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Replacing every cable and adapter simultaneously is the fastest way to identify cause.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring. Artifact under review: connector and end-to-end capability map.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Signal Path Test.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

checks.description: The decision applies Signal Path Test to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Signal Path Test.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and signal plus specific case evidence.
